title: tigridia pulchella
intro: Tigridia pulchella, also known as the Mexican flame flower or tiger flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It is native to Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds, as well as being used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Tigridia pulchella is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
